enrichment.short_description: Browser-based collaborative video editor with AI tools for subtitles, background removal, and smart cutting for creators and teams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Kapwing is a web-based video editing platform that runs entirely in the browser, letting anyone create, edit, and export videos without installing desktop software. Founded in 2017 by Julia Enthoven and Eric Lu, the company set out to make video editing as accessible as document editing in Google Docs, with real-time collaboration built into the core experience. Kapwing is headquartered in San Francisco and has grown into one of the more recognizable names among online video tools used by social media managers, marketers, and independent creators.

The platform combines a traditional timeline-based editor with a growing suite of AI-powered utilities, including automatic subtitle generation, background removal, silence trimming, and a Smart Cut tool that can identify and remove filler words or dead air from talking-head footage. Users can resize videos for different platforms, add text and captions, layer audio, and apply templates designed for short-form content like Reels, TikToks, and YouTube Shorts.

Because everything happens in the cloud, projects can be shared with a link the same way a Google Doc is shared, allowing multiple team members to comment, edit, or approve a video without exchanging large files. This collaborative, no-download approach is Kapwing's core differentiator against traditional desktop editors like Premiere Pro or Final Cut Pro, positioning it closer to a lightweight, team-friendly alternative for everyday social and marketing video work rather than a full professional post-production suite.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Kapwing Is For
paragraphs: Kapwing is best suited to social media managers, content marketers, and small teams who need to produce a steady volume of short, polished videos without deep editing expertise. Its browser-based nature means anyone with a laptop and internet connection can jump into a project, which makes it appealing to distributed or remote teams that need to collaborate on video without shared drives or file transfers.

Solo creators, podcasters repurposing audio into video clips, and educators building explainer content also find value in Kapwing's automated subtitle generation and quick trimming tools, which can turn a raw recording into a shareable clip in minutes. Marketing and social teams at small and mid-sized businesses use it to batch-produce platform-specific videos, since built-in templates and aspect-ratio presets for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube simplify repurposing one piece of content across channels.

Agencies managing multiple client accounts benefit from the workspace and sharing features, which let clients review drafts and leave feedback directly inside the editor rather than through email attachments. Overall, Kapwing fits best for people who value speed, accessibility, and collaboration over frame-by-frame editing precision.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Kapwing is not built for high-end film, broadcast, or complex multi-camera post-production work. Editors who need advanced color grading, precise audio mixing, multi-track compositing, or plugin ecosystems will find the toolset comparatively shallow next to desktop applications like Adobe Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, or Final Cut Pro.

Because Kapwing runs in the browser and renders in the cloud, performance can lag on very long or high-resolution projects, and users working with large raw footage libraries or offline environments may find the upload-and-process workflow slower than a local editing pipeline. Teams with unreliable internet connections or strict data-residency requirements for sensitive footage should also weigh this cloud dependency carefully.

Budget-conscious users who only need occasional, simple edits may find the free plan's watermark, short export length, and file size limits restrictive, and may prefer a one-time-purchase mobile app instead of committing to a recurring subscription. Similarly, users seeking a fully offline, install-once tool with no ongoing cost will likely be better served elsewhere.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: At its core, Kapwing offers a timeline-based video editor with trimming, layering, transitions, text overlays, and a library of stock media, music, and fonts that can be dropped directly into a project. Its AI-powered subtitle generator automatically transcribes spoken audio and syncs captions to the timeline, which can then be styled and edited manually, a feature especially popular for making social videos accessible and watchable on mute.

Smart Cut analyzes talking-head recordings to detect and remove silences or filler words automatically, speeding up the rough-cut process for podcasters and vloggers. Background removal uses AI to isolate subjects from their backgrounds without a green screen, useful for repositioning speakers over branded backgrounds or other footage. Kapwing also includes a resizer that reformats a single video into multiple aspect ratios for different platforms in one pass, along with templates for common formats like memes, Reels, and YouTube intros.

Collaboration tools sit alongside the editing features: shareable project links, commenting, and multi-user workspaces let teams review and approve videos together in real time. Additional utilities include a video-to-text transcriber, GIF maker, screen recorder, and a set of AI generation tools for scripts and images, rounding out Kapwing's positioning as an all-in-one content studio rather than a single-purpose editor.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Kapwing Experience
paragraphs: Using Kapwing feels closer to working in a cloud productivity app than a traditional editing suite. The interface opens in a browser tab with a familiar timeline at the bottom, a preview window in the center, and tool panels along the sides, so most editing actions are drag-and-drop or a click away rather than buried in nested menus. New users can typically start a basic edit within minutes without a tutorial.

The AI tools are generally the standout part of the day-to-day experience: uploading a clip and generating subtitles or trimming silence with Smart Cut happens with a single click, and results are usually good enough to use with only light manual correction. Rendering and export times depend on video length and current server load, which is the main trade-off users notice compared to local rendering on a desktop machine.

Free-plan users will regularly encounter the export watermark and length limits as a nudge toward upgrading, which can feel restrictive for repeated use but is manageable for occasional edits or testing the platform before committing to a subscription. Overall, the experience emphasizes speed and simplicity for short-form, social-first video production over depth for complex, long-form editing projects.

features.title: AI subtitle generator
features.description: Automatically transcribes spoken audio and syncs captions to the video timeline, with options to edit text and customize caption styling.

features.title: Smart Cut
features.description: Detects and removes silences, dead air, and filler words from talking-head recordings to speed up rough cuts.

features.title: AI background removal
features.description: Isolates a subject from its background without a physical green screen, letting users composite footage over new backgrounds.

features.title: Multi-format resizer
features.description: Reformats a single video into multiple aspect ratios for platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube in one export.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.description: Shareable project links let team members comment, edit, and review videos together, similar to collaborating on a shared document.

features.title: Timeline-based editor
features.description: Standard trimming, layering, transitions, and text overlay tools for building a video from uploaded or stock media.

features.title: Stock media and templates
features.description: A built-in library of stock video, images, music, and pre-built templates for common social and marketing formats.

features.title: Video-to-text transcription
features.description: Converts uploaded audio or video into an editable text transcript for repurposing or accessibility.

features.title: Screen recorder
features.description: Captures screen activity directly in the browser for tutorials, demos, or quick recordings without separate software.

features.title: Workspace and team management
features.description: Organizes projects into shared workspaces with member roles, useful for agencies and marketing teams managing multiple clients or campaigns.

pros: No software installation required; works entirely in a web browser
Strong AI tools for subtitles, background removal, and silence trimming that save editing time
Real-time collaboration and easy link-sharing for team and client review
Simple, approachable interface that's fast to learn for non-professional editors
Templates and one-click resizing make repurposing content across social platforms quick
cons: Free plan adds a watermark and limits exports to short lengths at lower resolution
Lacks the depth of professional tools like advanced color grading or multi-track audio mixing
Cloud-based rendering can be slower than local editing for long or high-resolution projects
Subscription cost can add up for teams that need multiple paid seats
Requires a stable internet connection since there is no full offline editing mode

faqs.question: Is Kapwing free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kapwing offers a free plan, but exports include a watermark and are limited in length and resolution. Removing the watermark and unlocking longer, higher-resolution exports requires a paid Pro or Business plan.

faqs.question: Does Kapwing work on mobile devices?
faqs.answer: Kapwing is primarily a browser-based tool that works on desktop and mobile web browsers, since it runs through the internet rather than as a native installed application.

faqs.question: How accurate are Kapwing's AI subtitles?
faqs.answer: Kapwing's automatic subtitle generator produces generally accurate transcriptions for clear audio, though users typically review and lightly edit the generated captions for names, accents, or background noise before finalizing.

faqs.question: Can multiple people edit a Kapwing project at the same time?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kapwing supports collaborative editing through shareable project links, allowing team members to comment on, review, and edit the same project similar to a shared document.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Kapwing's Pro and Business plans?
faqs.answer: The Business plan builds on Pro with a higher monthly credit allowance for AI tools like subtitling and background removal, along with expanded usage limits designed for teams producing a larger volume of video content.

use_cases.title: Repurposing podcast episodes into social clips
use_cases.description: Podcasters upload raw audio or video recordings and use Smart Cut and auto-subtitles to quickly produce short, captioned clips for social media.

use_cases.title: Batch-producing platform-specific social videos
use_cases.description: Marketing teams use the resizer and templates to reformat a single video into multiple aspect ratios for Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube.
use_cases.audience: Social media managers and marketing teams

use_cases.title: Collaborative client video review
use_cases.description: Agencies share project links with clients so they can review drafts and leave feedback directly within the editor before final export.

use_cases.title: Adding captions for accessibility and silent viewing
use_cases.description: Creators and businesses use the AI subtitle generator to add accurate captions to videos, improving accessibility and watchability without sound.

features: Exports limited to short video length at 720p
Exports up to 1080p and longer video lengths
Access to AI tools like subtitles, Smart Cut, and background removal
Larger file upload limit and cloud storage
Expanded subtitle and AI tool usage limits
Built for teams producing higher volumes of video

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kapwing is a browser-based video editor with AI subtitles, background removal, and Smart Cut, built for social teams and content creators.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered help desk with a unified SingleView customer timeline and pay-per-resolution ticket automation across email, chat, and social.
enrichment.starting_price: From $1 per AI-resolved ticket (no seat fees); full Kayako One platform is custom-priced

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Kayako is a customer support and help desk platform built around a single idea: agents should never have to hunt across tools to understand a customer's history. Its signature SingleView feature stitches together email, live chat, social messages, and past tickets into one continuous timeline, so support staff can pick up a conversation with full context regardless of which channel it started on.

The company has been through several eras since it first launched. What began as a traditional on-premise and cloud ticketing system has evolved into an AI-forward support platform, with the current product centered on Kay, an AI agent that can take real action on tickets, such as issuing refunds, updating orders, or resetting passwords, rather than just suggesting canned replies.

Kayako's newer pricing approach reflects that shift: instead of charging purely per agent seat, it bills per resolved ticket for AI-handled conversations, layered on top of a broader Kayako One platform that includes the inbox, ticketing, knowledge base, and reporting tools support teams expect from a modern help desk.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Kayako Is For
paragraphs: Kayako suits small to mid-sized support teams that handle a steady volume of repetitive requests, such as order status checks, password resets, or account changes, and want to offload a meaningful share of that volume to automation rather than adding headcount. Teams with a support inbox in the low thousands of tickets per month are a natural fit for its pay-per-resolution model.

It also appeals to companies already using tools like Shopify, Stripe, Salesforce, or Slack, since Kayako is built to plug into those systems so the AI agent can actually complete actions inside them, not just log a ticket about the problem. E-commerce and subscription businesses with well-defined, rules-based support workflows tend to see the clearest value.

Support leaders who want a single customer-history view without juggling separate chat, email, and social tools will also find Kayako's core proposition appealing, since SingleView was designed specifically to eliminate that kind of context-switching for agents.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Organizations that need fully transparent, self-serve pricing before talking to sales may find Kayako frustrating, since the full Kayako One platform is quoted on a custom basis and the headline pay-per-resolution rate only tells part of the cost story once platform and volume fees are added.

Very high-volume support operations should model costs carefully, because usage-based AI billing can scale unpredictably as ticket counts grow, in contrast to a flat per-agent fee that stays constant regardless of volume. Teams with highly unpredictable ticket spikes may prefer a predictable seat-based competitor instead.

Buyers who specifically want a large, well-known incumbent with the broadest possible app-marketplace and community ecosystem, such as Zendesk or Freshdesk, may find Kayako's smaller footprint and company size a drawback if breadth of pre-built integrations and third-party plugins matters more than depth of AI automation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: At the core of Kayako is its unified ticketing system, which brings email, live chat, and social conversations into one shared inbox, complete with collision detection so multiple agents don't accidentally respond to the same customer twice. Automation rules and SLA tools sit on top to route and prioritize tickets automatically.

The Kay AI agent extends that foundation by handling routine requests end-to-end, using integrations with commerce, CRM, and communication platforms to actually complete the action a customer asked for, rather than just drafting a reply for a human to send. A self-service knowledge base helps deflect simple questions before they become tickets at all.

Reporting and analytics dashboards let managers track resolution rates, response times, and agent workload, while the SingleView customer timeline remains the connective layer that ties every channel and interaction back to a single, chronological customer record for anyone who picks up the conversation.

long_form_blocks.heading: The Kayako Experience
paragraphs: Day to day, agents in Kayako work from a shared inbox that looks familiar to anyone who has used a modern help desk, but with the customer's full history, past purchases, and prior conversations visible alongside the current ticket, reducing the need to ask customers to repeat themselves.

For routine requests, the Kay AI agent often resolves the ticket before a human agent ever sees it, escalating only when a case falls outside its configured actions, which shifts the human team's day toward more complex, judgment-heavy conversations rather than repetitive lookups.

Setup and onboarding for the full platform tends to be a more guided, sales-assisted process than a self-service signup, reflecting Kayako's positioning toward mid-market and growing support teams that want a configured system tailored to their specific channels and integrations rather than a generic out-of-the-box tool.

features.title: SingleView customer timeline
features.description: Combines email, chat, social, and ticket history into one continuous view of each customer's interactions.

features.title: Kay AI agent
features.description: An AI agent that can take real actions on tickets, such as refunds, order updates, and password resets, not just draft replies.

features.title: Shared team inbox
features.description: A unified inbox for email, chat, and social messages with collision detection to prevent duplicate agent replies.

features.title: Live chat widget
features.description: Embeddable live chat for real-time conversations that feed directly into the same ticketing and customer view.

features.title: Self-service knowledge base
features.description: A searchable help center that lets customers resolve simple issues without opening a ticket.

features.title: Automation and SLA rules
features.description: Rule-based routing, prioritization, and service-level tracking to keep tickets moving to the right agent on time.

features.title: Reporting and analytics
features.description: Dashboards covering resolution rates, response times, and agent workload for support managers.

features.title: Third-party integrations
features.description: Connects with tools such as Slack, Stripe, Shopify, Salesforce, HubSpot, Twilio, and Zendesk, plus custom APIs.

features.title: Multi-channel ticketing
features.description: Consolidates email, chat, and social requests into a single ticketing workflow instead of separate tools.

pros: SingleView gives agents a genuinely unified history across channels instead of scattered records
The Kay AI agent can complete real actions, not just suggest responses, reducing manual work on routine tickets
Pay-per-resolution AI pricing has no seat or setup fees for that component
Solid range of integrations with commerce, CRM, and communication platforms
Long-established platform with a mature, purpose-built help desk feature set
cons: Full Kayako One platform pricing is custom and requires contacting sales rather than transparent self-serve rates
Usage-based AI billing can become harder to predict as ticket volume scales up
Smaller market presence and brand recognition compared to category leaders like Zendesk or Freshdesk
Onboarding for the full platform is more sales-assisted than instant self-signup
Limited publicly documented information on mobile app support compared to larger competitors

faqs.question: What is Kayako used for?
faqs.answer: Kayako is a help desk platform used by support teams to manage tickets, live chat, and knowledge base content from one unified inbox, with an AI agent that can automatically resolve routine requests.

faqs.question: How much does Kayako cost?
faqs.answer: Kayako's AI agent is billed per resolved ticket starting at $1 per resolution with no seat fees, while the full Kayako One platform is custom-priced based on your team's needs.

faqs.question: Does Kayako offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Kayako does not publicly advertise a self-serve free plan; pricing and trial access for the full platform are typically arranged by contacting sales.

faqs.question: What is SingleView in Kayako?
faqs.answer: SingleView is Kayako's feature that combines a customer's email, chat, social, and ticket history into one chronological timeline visible to any agent handling their case.

faqs.question: Does Kayako integrate with other tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, Kayako integrates with tools such as Slack, Stripe, Shopify, Salesforce, HubSpot, Twilio, Zendesk, and custom APIs to let its AI agent take action across systems.

use_cases.title: E-commerce order support
use_cases.description: Automatically resolving order status, refund, and shipping questions by connecting the AI agent to Shopify and Stripe.

use_cases.title: Unified multi-channel support desk
use_cases.description: Consolidating email, live chat, and social requests into one shared inbox with full customer history.
use_cases.audience: Growing support teams managing multiple channels

use_cases.title: AI-driven ticket deflection
use_cases.description: Using the Kay AI agent to resolve repetitive, rules-based tickets before they reach a human agent.
use_cases.audience: Support operations leaders focused on efficiency

use_cases.title: Account and access management support
use_cases.description: Handling password resets, account updates, and similar routine requests automatically via integrations.

features: AI agent completes actions like refunds and order updates
Integrates with Slack, Stripe, Shopify, Salesforce, and more
Billed only when a ticket is fully resolved
Knowledge base and live chat widget

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Kayako is an AI-driven help desk with a unified SingleView customer timeline, live chat, and pay-per-resolution ticket pricing.
